gaps: No existing test case covers device deletion, device list management, or post-deletion UI state verification.
No test case verifies that remaining devices retain correct name and connection status after a paired device is deleted.
No test case checks that settings remain accessible for remaining devices after deletion without requiring an app restart.
All existing test cases focus on onboarding, EULA, splash screen, and location permissions — none cover device management workflows.

generated_testcases.title: Verify remaining device retains correct name after deleting a paired device
steps: 1. Launch the app and complete onboarding (EULA, splash screen, location permissions).
2. Pair two devices (Device A and Device B) successfully.
3. Navigate to the device list screen.
4. Verify both Device A and Device B are displayed with correct names.
5. Select Device B and delete it using the delete/remove option.
6. Confirm deletion in the confirmation dialog.
7. Observe the remaining device (Device A) on the device list screen.
8. Check the name displayed for Device A.
generated_testcases.expected: The remaining device (Device A) displays its correct original name. It does NOT show the name of the deleted device (Device B).

generated_testcases.title: Verify remaining device retains correct connection status after deleting a paired device
steps: 1. Launch the app and complete onboarding.
2. Pair two devices (Device A and Device B) successfully.
3. Note the connection status of Device A (e.g., 'Connected' or 'Disconnected').
4. Delete Device B using the delete/remove option and confirm.
5. Observe the connection status of Device A on the device list screen.
generated_testcases.expected: The remaining device (Device A) retains its original connection status. The status does NOT change to reflect the deleted device's status.

generated_testcases.title: Verify settings remain accessible for remaining device after deletion without app restart
steps: 1. Launch the app and complete onboarding.
2. Pair two devices (Device A and Device B) successfully.
3. Delete Device B and confirm deletion.
4. Tap on the remaining device (Device A) to access its settings.
5. Interact with settings (e.g., toggle a setting, change a parameter).
6. Navigate back to the device list screen.
7. Tap on Device A again to access settings.
generated_testcases.expected: The settings screen for Device A opens successfully without any errors, freezes, or crashes. All settings are interactive and changes are applied correctly. No app restart is required.

generated_testcases.title: Verify device list correctly updates after deleting one of multiple paired devices
steps: 1. Launch the app and complete onboarding.
2. Pair three devices (Device A, Device B, Device C) successfully.
3. Navigate to the device list and verify all three are listed.
4. Delete Device B and confirm deletion.
5. Observe the device list.
generated_testcases.expected: The device list shows only Device A and Device C. Device B is no longer present. Both remaining devices display their correct names and connection statuses.

generated_testcases.title: Verify UI elements for remaining device are fully functional after deletion of another device
steps: 1. Launch the app and complete onboarding.
2. Pair two devices (Device A and Device B) successfully.
3. Delete Device B and confirm deletion.
4. For Device A, verify that all UI elements are responsive: tap on the device name, check connection status indicator, verify any edit/rename option works.
5. Verify that the delete/remove option for Device A is still available and functional.
generated_testcases.expected: All UI elements for Device A are fully functional and responsive. The device name is tappable, connection status is correctly displayed, edit/rename works, and the delete option is available and works correctly.

generated_testcases.title: Verify no stale data from deleted device appears in remaining device's details
steps: 1. Launch the app and complete onboarding.
2. Pair two devices (Device A and Device B) successfully.
3. Note the details of Device A (e.g., firmware version, battery level, signal strength).
4. Delete Device B and confirm deletion.
5. Tap on Device A to view its detailed information/settings.
6. Compare the displayed details with the previously noted details of Device A.
generated_testcases.expected: The details displayed for Device A match its own original data. No data from the deleted Device B (e.g., Device B's firmware version, battery level, or other attributes) is incorrectly shown for Device A.
